headline: Claude Desktop resource bloat raises deployment concerns for healthcare systems
body: Anthropic's Claude Desktop application spawns a 1.8 GB Hyper-V virtual machine on every launch, even when performing basic chat functions, according to reports verified across multiple developer communities. For healthcare organizations evaluating Claude for clinical documentation, EHR integration, or clinical decision support, this resource overhead creates practical deployment friction on enterprise infrastructure already stretched thin. The unnecessary VM instantiation increases attack surface, complicates endpoint management, and conflicts with healthcare IT's push toward lightweight, auditable tools in regulated environments. Healthcare IT leaders should document these resource requirements during vendor evaluation and factor infrastructure costs into total cost of ownership calculations for any Claude-based clinical workflow solutions.
